items.title: As the Sussexes return to Australia, 'unusual' commercial engagements are raising eyebrows

children.content: Harry and Meghan will be travelling under their titles of Duke and Duchess of Sussex, and will appear at two major, ticketed events as well as making the rounds of various charities and causes.

gallery.altText: prince harry and his wife Meghan leaving a cathedral in the UK smiling and happy.

thumbnail.altText: prince harry and his wife Meghan leaving a cathedral in the UK smiling and happy.

avatar.alt: A man with grey hair and beard in a blue suit and tie stands in a TV studio.

items.title: Terminally ill MP says ban on telehealth for assisted dying must change

children.content: A Labor politician living with MND has written to the attorney-general, calling for laws banning doctors from discussing voluntary assisted dying during telehealth appointments to be revised.

image.altText: Woman in read seated on set of stairs wearing a blue beanie.

items.title: Memorial for women allegedly killed by men removed from Melbourne laneway

children.content: The founder of Australian Femicide Watch says she is heartbroken over the removal of the She Matters mural in Hosier Lane dedicated to more than 200 women who have died since January 2025.

image.altText: A person stands at the top of a ladder against a wall with women's pictures as a crowd gathers below.

items.title: Bomb squad called in after rental home cleaner finds explosives, drugs, cash

children.content: Authorities were forced to conduct controlled explosions at a property in Melbourne's north-west after a cleaner stumbled across drugs and explosives.

items.title: Teen who 'weaponised' stolen cars denied bail over risk to community

children.content: A 13-year-old girl is facing court over 109 charges relating to incidents including allegedly driving into a cyclist.

image.altText: Police motorbikes

items.title: Health minister and veteran MPs step down before Victorian state election

children.content: Long serving Labor MPs Mary-Anne Thomas, Danny Pearson and Gayle Tierney have announced they will not contest the 2026 state election.

image.altText: Mary Anne Thomas, Danny Pearson, Gayle Tierney

items.title: Backyard astronomer who helped to discover 100 distant planets awarded medal

children.content: From a backyard observatory in regional Victoria, Gippsland astronomer Chris Stockdale spends his nights helping confirm distant planets by tracking tiny changes in starlight that reveal new worlds beyond our solar system.

image.altText: A man wearing glasses and a blue button-down shirt, holding a box and grabbing a large telescope

items.title: Victorian government tried to keep major housing budget blowout under wraps

children.content: Internal documents the Victorian government tried to withhold reveal that a landmark social housing construction project was facing a significant budget blowout two years after it was unveiled.

image.altText: aerial shot of melbourne showing houses and city skyscrapers

items.title: Caretaker files for possession of impressionist Frederick McCubbin's former country home

children.content: A court has heard claims the former home of an Australian impressionist is not safe to walk through, with "black mould, evidence of vermin and wheelie bins collecting rainwater".

gallery.altText: A two-storey green house with brick chimneys.

thumbnail.altText: A two-storey green house with brick chimneys.

items.title: Farmer braces for backlash after speaking up in support of transmission lines

children.content: A farmer in Victoria's north is offering up his property for transmission lines as part of the VNI West project, saying it would give him a chance to diversify his income.

image.altText: A transmission tower and power lines over a farm

items.title: Hiker found 'safe and well' after spending 24 hours lost in Victoria's north-west

children.content: Police have located a hiker who separated from his group and was missing for more than 24 hours in Victoria's north-west.

image.altText: Police finds missing camper Andy

items.title: The recycling project turning used tennis balls into shoes

children.content: What should be done with all those used tennis balls that get inevitably sent to landfill? Lilian Xu might have found the answer.

gallery.altText: Lilian Xu sits with red shoes next to tennis balls in the stands of Kooyng Tennis Club.

thumbnail.altText: Lilian Xu sits with red shoes next to tennis balls in the stands of Kooyng Tennis Club.

avatar.alt: A man smiles as he presents the television news.

items.title: Meet the teens learning maths, English and winemaking at school

children.content: These high school students in Corowa make up to 750 bottles of wine a year as part of their studies.

image.altText: A 14 year old boy stands in a vineyard looking at grapes in his hands.

items.title: Regional Australia leads GP bulk-billing surge

children.content: A new report shows regional and remote towns are rapidly lifting their GP bulk-billing rates while out-of-pocket costs rise across the country.

image.altText: generic photo of psychologist's hand holding a pen and notepad with a patient

items.title: Police appeal for information over mysterious Ouyen death

children.content: Richard Wills was found shot and buried in a shallow grave at his rural property in Victoria's north-west earlier this week.

image.altText: Man wearing black cap holding a baby

items.title: Opposition plans to recruit overseas officers to fill Victoria Police vacancies

children.content: The opposition says officers from the UK, Ireland and New Zealand would be offered $5,000 to relocate under a plan to recruit 3,000 new officers.

image.altText: Four police officers in high-vis uniforms, with their backs to the camera.

items.title: 'Cosplay is not consent': The rules of dressing up for conventions

children.content: Cosplayers say their hobby has had an astronomical rise in popularity over the past decade. While the subculture promotes "no limits" creativity, there are still strict rules.

avatar.alt: A man smiles as he presents the television news.

items.title: Two people seriously injured in Melbourne home invasion

children.content: Victoria Police says three or four males in their late teens or early 20s wore masks when they broke into a Greenvale home.

image.altText: A night time image of a house.

items.title: Country church with lone parishioner to close as modern worship evolves

children.content: Anglican priest David Perryman delivers Communion to a congregation of one in a small town about three hours east of Melbourne — but not for much longer.

image.altText: David Perryman

items.title: Snoozing seal blocks road in popular coastal spot

children.content: Locals say they are not surprised the seal, described as a regular visitor, decided to stop for a snooze on the roadway.

image.altText: A seal sleeping on a road.

items.title: Collard's extraordinary AFL disciplinary hearing sets new precedent

children.content: In an AFL first, St Kilda's Lance Collard has faced a disciplinary tribunal hearing for conduct unbecoming.

image.altText: GettyImages-2231737279

items.title: Controversial fence dividing dog owners and environmentalists removed

children.content: A fence designed to keep dogs out of a park in Melbourne's north has been removed after community opposition.

gallery.altText: The words stop the fence spray painted on a tree.

thumbnail.altText: The words stop the fence spray painted on a tree.

items.title: Fresh push for Murray Basin freight rail to get more produce off trucks

children.content: North-west Victorian farmers are pushing for the completion of the Murray Basin Rail Project, more than seven years after it was originally slated to be finished.

image.altText: old broad gauge rail track near Manangatang in Victoria's west

items.title: Families demand refunds for Easter carnival that left 'kids in tears'

children.content: A Melbourne council is investigating an Easter event held at the Werribee racecourse after it was criticised by attendees, who said it felt unsafe, underwhelming and poorly organised.

image.altText: A large number of children and adults search through hay, holding baskets on a sunny day.

items.title: Police failures exposed during inquest into 50 year cold case

children.content: Ms Green was last seen alive on February 13, 1976, with the Coroner's Court of Victoria hearing the litany of policing errors following her disappearance.

gallery.altText: A black and white photo of Veronica Green who wears a long white dress.

thumbnail.altText: A black and white photo of Veronica Green who wears a long white dress.

avatar.alt: A man with dark skin in a suit looks at the camera

items.title: Victoria's 2026 election to host Australia's largest trial of low-sensory voting

children.content: State election voting will be made quieter and calmer for neurodivergent voters in Victoria.

image.altText: A man in chinos and a tshirt with a lanyard leans over to cast a vote at an election

items.title: Man pleads guilty to dealing with millions of dollars from illegal tobacco

children.content: Malek Alhmedai's hire car had $4 million stashed inside, with the money linked to illegal tobacco sales.

image.altText: Boxes of cigarettes surrounded by Australian cash.

items.title: Farmers angered by plans to turn coal mine into lake bigger than Sydney Harbour

children.content: There are plans to turn the former Hazelwood coal mine in Victoria into a lake bigger than Sydney Harbour, but farmers and environmentalists are worried about the impact on nearby waterways.

gallery.altText: A young man with a beard, wearing a blue shirt and cap stands alongside a riverbed.

thumbnail.altText: A young man with a beard, wearing a blue shirt and cap stands alongside a riverbed.

items.title: Racism erupts after Melbourne announces Little India plans

children.content: Social media posts about the announcement of a Little India precinct in Melbourne are flooded with racist comments, leaving some Indians with mixed feelings about the plan.

image.altText: Purva wearing a pink sari while speaking on stage at a panel

items.title: Hundreds more PSOs at Melbourne stations if Liberals win state election

children.content: The Victorian opposition is promising to recruit hundreds more protective service officers if it wins government in November.

image.altText: Three protective service officers wearing masks walk down a footpath in Melbourne's CBD.

items.title: Cyclist clinging to reeds, pulled to safety in remote Snowy River rescue

children.content: The cyclist was clinging to reeds in the middle of the Snowy River when the police air wing pulled them to safety.

image.altText: Black and white image of a cyclist in a river
